headline: Iran Update: 10 Days In, Trump Says Iran War Could End 'Very Soon'
summary: President Trump said Monday the war in Iran could end 'very soon' but threatened harder strikes if oil flows get disrupted. Oil prices spiked close to $120 per barrel.

reactions.quote: 10 days in and we're already talking about it ending 'very soon'? That's the same language we heard about Afghanistan in 2001. I hope I'm wrong.
The strike package has been devastatingly effective. Iran's nuclear enrichment capacity is functionally destroyed. The question now is whether we have the political will to finish what we started.
Americans are paying $6 for gas while the president says the war could end 'very soon.' Where have we heard this before? The rest of the world is watching and they've seen this movie.
Who benefits from $120 oil? Not the American middle class. Every war has its profiteers. Follow the money and you'll find the real reason we're in Iran.
There is no congressional authorization for this war. The War Powers Act exists for a reason. 'Very soon' is not a military strategy, it's a campaign slogan.
Trump is doing what Obama never had the courage to do. Iran has been the #1 state sponsor of terror for 45 years. It's about time someone dealt with it decisively.
We are bombing a country of 88 million people and the discourse is about gas prices. 88 million people. Think about that number for a second.
Spoke with two Iran experts today. Both said the same thing: destroying nuclear facilities is the easy part. What comes after is what no one in Washington is talking about.
Every generation gets its war. The question young Americans should be asking: who voted for this? Because I don't remember it being on any ballot.
Gas is at $6. Groceries already had people struggling. And now we're in another war nobody asked for. At what point do we start asking who's paying for this? Because it's us.
I left Iran as a kid. My family fled that regime. I understand why people want it gone. But regime change from the outside has never worked. Not once. The Iranian people have to do it themselves.
Oil at $120 means everything gets more expensive. Shipping, food, utilities. If you're 22 and already barely making rent, this war is about to hit your bank account.
